concepts.finding: Framework for large-scale graph embedding (billions of nodes, trillions of edges). Distributed training with graph partitioning, negative sampling (uniform, adversarial, or filtered), and configurable loss functions (margin ranking, logistic). Handles multi-relation graphs. Trains 100M-node graph in <1 day on single machine.
concepts.implication: PBG provides the infrastructure for billion-node keyword embeddings. Multi-relation support allows different relationship types (co-occurrence, search session, click-through). Adversarial negative sampling improves embedding quality.
concepts.open_question: What graph embedding framework (PBG, DGL-KE, GraphVite) scales best for million-node keyword-intent graphs?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: PBG-trained keyword embeddings achieve similar cluster quality to single-machine methods while scaling to 100x more keywords.
